notes: An aider turn aborts on a DETERMINISTIC, non-faked, NON-retryable provider error — NO mock/proxy needed (this is why the aider row, like the pi sibling, does not reuse the claudecode mock-anthropic-5xx path; that path is `claude --bare` + ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and does not transfer to aider's litellm stack). The interactive driver launches aider in a tmux REPL with IRRLICHT_AIDER_MODEL=<bogus-model> (a structurally-invalid / unsupported model id), sends one prompt, and the provider returns an unsupported/invalid-model error whose message does NOT match aider's litellm retry class — aider does not retry, prints a `> …Error`/`> …Exception` blockquote to .aider.chat.history.md, and the turn aborts WITHOUT a normal `> Tokens:` end-of-call line. AIDER-SPECIFIC observability (see assessment.json + core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go): aider is the lone FilesUnderCWD adapter — its transcript is markdown parsed by a RawParser. With a turn open (`####` user line seen), the parser's flushErrorTurn (#262) matches the error blockquote via errorRE (`^> \\s* \\S* (?:Error|Exception)`) and emits a synthetic turn_done carrying the error as AssistantText (Tokens/Contribution nil — the aborted call reported no completed API usage). The classifier's 'agent finished turn → ready' rule settles working→ready off that turn_done. IdleFlush (1500ms transcript-quiet) is the sweep BACKSTOP: even if a provider surfaced the failure under a word that errorRE missed, an open-then-quiet turn still flushes to turn_done within ~max(1500ms IdleFlush floor, the daemon's 5s SessionDetector staleWorking tick). The turn does NOT stick in working. NOTE on the scenario's 'sweep' primitive: there is no working-state idle sweep in the daemon today (PR #106 removed the stale-tool timer); aider reaches the same user-observable end-state (resolves to ready, no stick in working) via the error epilogue (flushErrorTurn), with IdleFlush as the inactivity backstop — so this fixture asserts that END-STATE, not an actual sweep transition. SESSION-IDENTITY (same workaround as aider/interrupted-turn + multi-turn-conversation): aider's transcript is lazy, so at launch the process scanner emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready — session_birth, which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch', legitimately matches that pre-session. turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the per-cwd rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); every later phase, the pid_bind, and the teardown then pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. RECIPE SHAPE: interactive (NOT headless) — aider's error settle rides IdleFlush + the 5s staleWorking poll, so the aider process must outlive that window; a headless `aider --message` would exit non-zero the instant it writes the error blockquote and truncate the working→ready settle and the teardown observation. The trailing sleep after the prompt is therefore ≥6s (one IdleFlush cycle plus the 5s poll plus slack) before the driver tears the REPL down. HOW runs differ: the exact provider error wording (litellm's BadRequest/NotFound/Unsupported message), whether any banner/prose precedes it, per-run sub-second timing, and the fresh per-run UUID/PID all vary; structurally stable is one ready→working→ready arc on one rollout session_id that never enters waiting, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (aider's transcript is lazy, so this is the launch-time proc-<PID> pre-session)
User prompt → working (binds the per-cwd rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)

aider binds the PID at session birth (~ms after launch, BEFORE the first prompt), so anchor to recording start and do not pin same_session_as — same shape as aider/basic-turn and aider/interrupted-turn

The model call aborts on a non-retryable provider error: aider writes a `> …Error`/`> …Exception` blockquote with no normal `> Tokens:` end-of-call line, and the parser's flushErrorTurn synthesizes a turn_done (IdleFlush is the inactivity backstop) → the turn resolves back to ready; state does not stick in working and never entered waiting
The REPL is torn down once after the aborted turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
